name: Fish Stock
recipeIngredient: 9 lb Fish bones (from any firm-fleshed whitefish such as Sea bass, Rockfish, or Halibut)
1 cup Coarsely chopped leeks (white part only)
4 Sprigs fresh thyme OR 1/2 tbsp dried thyme
4 Garlic cloves, lightly crushed and left unpeeled
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the fish bones well under cold running water.
2. Place the bones in a large stockpot and cover with water. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
3. Cook for 10 minutes, skimming frequently.
4. Add the remaining ingredients and simmer for 1 hour.
5. Remove the fish bones and vegetables using a slotted spoon.
6. Strain the stock through a cheesecloth-lined strainer.
7. Allow the stock to cool and skim the surface.
8. Divide into small containers and freeze for future use.
Makes 4 Quarts
